title: citrus medica var. limonum
intro: Citrus medica var. limonum, also known as the lemon, is a variety of sour orange (Citrus medica) that is widely cultivated for its fruit, which is one of the most commonly consumed citrus fruits globally. The lemon has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and infections. The health benefits of lemon include boosting the immune system, aiding in weight loss, and reducing the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The herbal preparations of lemon include lemon juice, essential oil, and dried peel, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications for skin and wound care.
